primary.title: Keyword Density Checker - Analyze Word Frequencies Online
primary.description: Analyze and calculate keyword density of any web page or text copy. Filter out stop words to optimize SEO content and avoid stuffing. Free online tool.
primary.heading: Keyword Density Checker
primary.seo_content:
A Keyword Density Checker is a text parsing tool used by SEO copywriters and content editors to count how many times words or multi-word phrases appear in an article. It displays the density percentage (ratio of keyword occurrence to total word count) to help search engine optimization experts audit copy before publishing.

Optimal keyword density is generally between 1.0% and 2.5%. Going above 3.5% can flag search algorithms for 'keyword stuffing' which negatively impacts rankings. Stripping common 'stop words' (words like *the*, *and*, *is*) is critical to see the true subject matter focus of a page.

examples.title: Analyze keyword density for a landing page
examples.description: Paste your page content into the editor, check the 'Ignore Stop Words' box, and review the 1, 2, and 3-word phrase tables to verify your main topics are highlighted.

examples.title: Check article for keyword stuffing
examples.description: Review the density column. If any commercial search term shows a density badge above 3.5%, re-write sentences to incorporate synonyms or pronouns.

faqs.question: What is the formula for keyword density?
faqs.answer: Keyword density is calculated as: (Number of occurrences of the keyword ÷ Total number of words in the analyzed text) × 100.

faqs.question: What are stop words in keyword density checker?
faqs.answer: Stop words are high-frequency grammatical connector words (like 'a', 'the', 'of', 'in', 'and'). Removing them allows the analyzer to focus exclusively on content-rich terms that relate to search intent.

keyword-frequency-analyzer.title: Keyword Frequency Analyzer - Count Word Occurrences
keyword-frequency-analyzer.description: Analyze text blocks to count word and phrase occurrences instantly. Perfect for copy editing, transcript parsing, and subject auditing.
keyword-frequency-analyzer.heading: Keyword Frequency Analyzer
keyword-frequency-analyzer.seo_content: The Keyword Frequency Analyzer provides a detailed breakdown of absolute occurrence counts for 1-word, 2-word, and 3-word phrases. It helps content creators audit vocabulary variety and improve textual flow.

examples.title: Audit transcript vocabulary
examples.description: Paste an interview transcript to find the most commonly discussed subjects and clean up redundant verbal ticks.

faqs.question: What are 2-word and 3-word phrases in text analysis?
faqs.answer: These are adjacent word sequences (also called bigrams and trigrams). Analyzing multi-word phrases reveals key compound terms (like 'digital marketing' or 'search engine optimization') that 1-word analysis would split up.

keyword-stuffing-checker.title: Keyword Stuffing Checker - Avoid Search Penalties
keyword-stuffing-checker.description: Audit your copywriting with a real-time keyword stuffing alert system. Ensure natural phrasing for optimal search engine performance.
keyword-stuffing-checker.heading: Keyword Stuffing Checker
keyword-stuffing-checker.seo_content: The Keyword Stuffing Checker highlights keywords exceeding safe density limits. Modern search engines like Google penalize pages that unnaturally stuff words into paragraphs to manipulate search rankings.

examples.title: Pre-publish SEO compliance check
examples.description: Paste draft copy prior to uploading to your CMS. Flag and replace overused key terms with natural synonyms if a warning triggers.

faqs.question: How do search engines detect keyword stuffing?
faqs.answer: Search engines use advanced natural language processing (NLP) models like BERT and MUM to verify if words are used in context. Repeatedly listing keywords out of grammatical context is easily flagged.
